labor.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: 60 federal penalties totaling $89.7M since 2000 (Violation Tracker).

charity.s: $70M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.08% of revenue) via P&G Fund.

dei.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. Disability:IN Equality Index score 100/100. JUST Capital JUST 100 — ranked #28 of Russell 1000 (2025) for treatment of workers, customers, communities, environment. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture. Held by 1 ESG/SRI fund(s) (gender-equality-funds) — investor-screen positive signal. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

animals.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: Wikipedia "Animal testing" section: Procter & Gamble has received criticism from animal advocacy group PETA for the practice of testing on animals. On June 30, 1999, Procter & Gamble announced that it would limit its animal testing…

privacy.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: CFPB consumer complaints: 390 on record (33 in past 12mo); 100% timely response rate.

execPay.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 276:1 (median employee pay $79,510) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2025).

wiki.extract: Pantene is an American brand of hair care products now owned by Procter & Gamble. The product line originated in Europe in 1945 by Hoffmann-La Roche of Switzerland, which based the name on panthenol. It started to be introduced in the United States and around the world in 1985 when it was purchased by Richardson Vicks (Vicks) of the United States. That company was taken over in the same year by the American company Procter & Gamble (P&G) in order for P&G to compete in the "beauty product" market rather than only functional products.

cases.caseName: MURPHY MOTOR FREIGHT v. PANTENE CO.

health.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: 4 CPSC recalls (1 in past 24 months); latest hazard: the handheld hair dryers lack an immersion protection device.
